text: Here's what science actually says about randomized controlled trials. Randomized controlled trials, or RCTs, are the gold standard in research for testing new treatments. Participants are randomly assigned to a treatment or a control group, minimizing bias. This method ensures that any difference in outcomes is likely due to the treatment itself. But not all studies are RCTs, and preliminary research can mislead us. Understanding RCTs is crucial for making informed health decisions. Want to dive deeper into how science shapes your health? Hit subscribe!
